subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child understands how regions help organize and describe Earth's surface.
Your child uses tools to identify, describe, and draw special four-sided shapes and sorts flat shapes based on their features.
Your child can observe and describe shapes by their important features, sort and draw them, and begin learning about fractions by dividing shapes.
Your child uses tools to explore the features of triangles and circles.
Your child can locate all the continents on a map or globe.
Your child understands how Gestalt ideas help us see and understand things as whole patterns.
Your child can find important lines on maps and globes like the prime meridian, equator, Tropics of Capricorn and Cancer, International Date Line, and lines of latitude and longitude.
Your child learns about shapes by looking at their parts and how they fit together, and practices dividing shapes to understand fractions.
canonical_name: Grade 5 Language Arts: Geography — Map Skills and Earth’s Regions
coherence_reason: This cluster contains standards about geography (regions, map skills), geometry (shapes, fractions), and even Gestalt psychology, which are clearly different topics.
split_topics: Geometry: Shapes, Features, and Introduction to Fractions

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child reads grade-level texts with purpose and understanding.
Your child can tell what the narrator or speaker believes during a story or speech.
Your child can compare two people, events, or ideas in something they read.
With help, your child can use technology, like the Internet, to write and work with others.
Your child can pick out one or more details from information they hear or see in other media.
Your child can find information in books or on the computer.
Your child compares stories in the same genre by looking at how they handle similar themes and topics.
Your child can write about what they think or feel about a topic or something they read.
canonical_name: Grade 5 Language Arts: Reading Comprehension — Analyzing and Comparing Texts
coherence_reason: The standards cover related skills in reading comprehension and text analysis, but also include technology use and writing, which are distinct enough to consider splitting.

subject: Language Arts
sample_standards: Your child understands how saving money and investing money are connected and how they work together.
Your child can review financial products and services to see if they fit specific goals.
Your child knows that contracts are promises people must keep.
Your child understands that people decide how to spend money based on what they have, what they need, and what they want.
Your child can name products people use at banks and other financial places.
Your child can use different ways to build wealth and increase what they own.
Your child can compare different financial services and products to see which fit their goals.
Your child can explain where to find help and protection as a consumer, including public groups and private experts or websites.
canonical_name: Grade 5 Language Arts: Personal Finance — Financial Decision-Making and Consumer Awareness
coherence_reason: All standards focus on personal finance skills, but some emphasize financial products/services while others highlight decision-making, contracts, and consumer protection.
